name: eval-2-migration-plan-existing-repo
query: Plan the migration of my DTST app from Tauri to a local-first Node/Express web app. The repo is ryankolean/dtst. Produce a Dispatch plan.
context_setup: Existing repo has Tauri + React frontend, SQLite backing store, and a specific folder structure under src/. The migration needs to preserve data, migrate the frontend to plain Vite, replace Tauri commands with HTTP routes, and introduce Express without breaking the existing UI.
expected_behavior: Phase 1 (Repo Analysis): produces a Code Report of the existing Tauri structure — identifies the Tauri commands that need HTTP equivalents, the data store layer, and the frontend touch points
Phase 2 (Architecture and Design): lays out the target Node/Express architecture and the migration bridge (how data and routes get translated one-for-one)

Phase 3 (Task Decomposition): produces sequential Dispatch tasks ordered for safe migration — typically scaffold Express → port data layer → port routes one-by-one → swap frontend HTTP client → remove Tauri → verify
Tasks are small enough to be reviewed individually — no 'migrate everything' mega-task
Each task names the specific files it touches and the verification criterion (tests pass, route responds, UI still renders)
Plan includes a rollback strategy or explicit statement that the migration is staged so each step is reversible
Produces the structured markdown execution file
failure_modes_to_check: Skips Phase 1 repo analysis and plans against assumed structure
Produces tasks too coarse for Dispatch workers to execute safely
No ordering or dependency awareness (tasks could be run in parallel even though they conflict)
